
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:120:     * Generates email links and OTPs to be sent via a custom email provider.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:121:     * @param email The user's email.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:122:     * @param options.password User password. For signup only.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:123:     * @param options.data Optional user metadata. For signup only.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:124:     * @param options.redirectTo The redirect url which should be appended to the generated link
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:125:     *
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:127:     *
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:128:     * @remarks
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:129:     * - The following types can be passed into `generateLink()`: `signup`, `magiclink`, `invite`, `recovery`, `email_change_current`, `email_change_new`, `phone_change`.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:130:     * - `generateLink()` only generates the email link for `email_change_email` if the **Secure email change** is enabled in your project's [email auth provider settings](/dashboard/project/_/auth/providers).
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:131:     * - `generateLink()` handles the creation of the user for `signup`, `invite` and `magiclink`.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:132:     *

  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:138:     *   password: 'secret'
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueAdminApi.d.ts:140:     * ```

  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:203:     * error message that attempts to hide this information from the user.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:204:     * This method has support for PKCE via email signups. The PKCE flow cannot be used when autoconfirm is enabled.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:205:     *
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:206:     * @returns A logged-in session if the server has "autoconfirm" ON
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:215:     *   - If **Confirm email** is disabled, both a `user` and a `session` are returned.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:216:     * - When the user confirms their email address, they are redirected to the [`SITE_URL`](/docs/guides/auth/redirect-urls#use-wildcards-in-redirect-urls) by default. > node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:217:     * - If signUp() is called for an existing confirmed user:
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:218:     *   - When both **Confirm email** and **Confirm phone** (even when phone provider is disabled) are enabled in [your project](/dashboard/project/_/auth/providers), an obfuscated/fake user object is returned.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:219:     *   - When either **Confirm email** or **Confirm phone** (even when phone provider is disabled) is disabled, the error message, `User already registered` is returned.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:222:     * @example Sign up with an email and password

> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:376:    signUp(credentials: SignUpWithPasswordCredentials): Promise<AuthResponse>;
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:377:    /**
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:378:     * Log in an existing user with an email and password or phone and password.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:946:     * - Requires either an email or phone number.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:947:     * - This method is used for passwordless sign-ins where a OTP is sent to the user's email or phone number.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:948:     * - If the user doesn't exist, `signInWithOtp()` will signup the user instead. To restrict this behavior, you can set `shouldCreateUser` in `SignInWithPasswordlessCredentials.options` to `false`.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:949:     * - If you're using an email, you can configure whether you want the user to receive a magiclink or a OTP.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:950:     * - If you're using phone, you can configure whether you want the user to receive a OTP.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1012:     *   1. `sms` – Used when verifying a one-time password (OTP) sent via SMS during sign-up or sign-in.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1013:     *   2. `phone_change` – Used when verifying an OTP sent to a new phone number during a phone number update process.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1014:     * - If an email address is used, the type can be one of the following (note: `signup` and `magiclink` types are deprecated):
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1015:     *   1. `email` – Used when verifying an OTP sent to the user's email during sign-up or sign-in.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1016:     *   2. `recovery` – Used when verifying an OTP sent for account recovery, typically after a password reset request.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1020:     * - The `TokenHash` is contained in the [email templates](/docs/guides/auth/auth-email-templates) and can be used to sign in.  You may wish to use the hash for the PKCE flow for Server Side Auth. Read [the Password-based Auth guide](/docs/guides/auth/passwords) for more details.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1021:     *

> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1225:     * - Resends a signup confirmation, email change or phone change email to the user.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1226:     * - Passwordless sign-ins can be resent by calling the `signInWithOtp()` method again.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1227:     * - Password recovery emails can be resent by calling the `resetPasswordForEmail()` method again.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1228:     * - This method will only resend an email or phone OTP to the user if there was an initial signup, email change or phone change request being made(note: For existing users signing in with OTP, you should use `signInWithOtp()` again to resend the OTP).
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1229:     * - You can specify a redirect url when you resend an email link using the `emailRedirectTo` option.

  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1621:     * - Requires either an email or phone number.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1622:     * - This method is used for passwordless sign-ins where a OTP is sent to the user's email or phone number.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1623:     * - If the user doesn't exist, `signInWithOtp()` will signup the user instead. To restrict this behavior, you can set `shouldCreateUser` in `SignInWithPasswordlessCredentials.options` to `false`.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1624:     * - If you're using an email, you can configure whether you want the user to receive a magiclink or a OTP.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1625:     * - If you're using phone, you can configure whether you want the user to receive a OTP.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1738:     *   1. `sms` – Used when verifying a one-time password (OTP) sent via SMS during sign-up or sign-in.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1739:     *   2. `phone_change` – Used when verifying an OTP sent to a new phone number during a phone number update process.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1740:     * - If an email address is used, the type can be one of the following (note: `signup` and `magiclink` types are deprecated):
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1741:     *   1. `email` – Used when verifying an OTP sent to the user's email during sign-up or sign-in.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1742:     *   2. `recovery` – Used when verifying an OTP sent for account recovery, typically after a password reset request.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1746:     * - The `TokenHash` is contained in the [email templates](/docs/guides/auth/auth-email-templates) and can be used to sign in.  You may wish to use the hash for the PKCE flow for Server Side Auth. Read [the Password-based Auth guide](/docs/guides/auth/passwords) for more details.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\main\GoTrueClient.js:1747:     *

  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:6:export default class GoTrueClient {
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:7:    private static nextInstanceID;
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:202:     * Be aware that if a user account exists in the system you may get back an
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:203:     * error message that attempts to hide this information from the user.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:204:     * This method has support for PKCE via email signups. The PKCE flow cannot be used when autoconfirm is enabled.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:205:     *
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:206:     * @returns A logged-in session if the server has "autoconfirm" ON
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:215:     *   - If **Confirm email** is disabled, both a `user` and a `session` are returned.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:216:     * - When the user confirms their email address, they are redirected to the [`SITE_URL`](/docs/guides/auth/redirect-urls#use-wildcards-in-redirect-urls) by default. You can modify your `SITE_URL` or add additional redirect URLs in [your project](/dashboard/project/_/auth/url-configuration).
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:217:     * - If signUp() is called for an existing confirmed user:
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:218:     *   - When both **Confirm email** and **Confirm phone** (even when phone provider is disabled) are enabled in [your project](/dashboard/project/_/auth/providers), an obfuscated/fake user object is returned.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:219:     *   - When either **Confirm email** or **Confirm phone** (even when phone provider is disabled) is disabled, the error message, `User already registered` is returned.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:222:     * @example Sign up with an email and password
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:223:     * ```js
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:224:     * const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:225:     *   email: 'example@email.com',
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:226:     *   password: 'example-password',
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:319:     * @example Sign up with a phone number and password (SMS)
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:320:     * ```js
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:321:     * const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:322:     *   phone: '123456789',
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:323:     *   password: 'example-password',
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:333:     * @example Sign up with a phone number and password (whatsapp)
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:334:     * ```js
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:335:     * const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({

  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:344:     * @example Sign up with additional user metadata

  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:375:     */
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:376:    signUp(credentials: SignUpWithPasswordCredentials): Promise<AuthResponse>;
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:377:    /**
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:378:     * Log in an existing user with an email and password or phone and password.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:946:     * - Requires either an email or phone number.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:947:     * - This method is used for passwordless sign-ins where a OTP is sent to the user's email or phone number.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:948:     * - If the user doesn't exist, `signInWithOtp()` will signup the user instead. To restrict this behavior, you can set `shouldCreateUser` in `SignInWithPasswordlessCredentials.options` to `false`.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:949:     * - If you're using an email, you can configure whether you want the user to receive a magiclink or a OTP.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:950:     * - If you're using phone, you can configure whether you want the user to receive a OTP.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1012:     *   1. `sms` – Used when verifying a one-time password (OTP) sent via SMS during sign-up or sign-in.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1013:     *   2. `phone_change` – Used when verifying an OTP sent to a new phone number during a phone number update process.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1014:     * - If an email address is used, the type can be one of the following (note: `signup` and `magiclink` types are deprecated):
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1015:     *   1. `email` – Used when verifying an OTP sent to the user's email during sign-up or sign-in.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1016:     *   2. `recovery` – Used when verifying an OTP sent for account recovery, typically after a password reset request.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1020:     * - The `TokenHash` is contained in the [email templates](/docs/guides/auth/auth-email-templates) and can be used to sign in.  You may wish to use the hash for the PKCE flow for Server Side Auth. Read [the Password-based Auth guide](/docs/guides/auth/passwords) for more details.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1021:     *
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1022:     * @example Verify Signup One-Time Password (OTP)
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1023:     * ```js
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1024:     * const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.verifyOtp({ email, token, type: 'email'})
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1025:     * ```
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1026:     *
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1027:     * @exampleResponse Verify Signup One-Time Password (OTP)
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1028:     * ```json
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1029:     * {
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1218:    private _reauthenticate;
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1219:    /**
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1220:     * Resends an existing signup confirmation email, email change email, SMS OTP or phone change OTP.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1221:     *
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1222:     * @category Auth
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1223:     *
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1224:     * @remarks
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1225:     * - Resends a signup confirmation, email change or phone change email to the user.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1226:     * - Passwordless sign-ins can be resent by calling the `signInWithOtp()` method again.
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1227:     * - Password recovery emails can be resent by calling the `resetPasswordForEmail()` method again.
> node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1228:     * - This method will only resend an email or phone OTP to the user if there was an initial signup, email change or phone change request being made(note: For existing users signing in with OTP, you should use `signInWithOtp()` again to resend the OTP).
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.d.ts:1229:     * - You can specify a redirect url when you resend an email link using the `emailRedirectTo` option.

  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\GoTrueClient.js:2064:     * const { error } = await supabase.auth.resend({
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\lib\error-codes.d.ts:4: * on the server).
  node_modules\@supabase\auth-js\dist\module\lib\error-codes.d.ts:5: */
